# Notion SDK for Java

> A typed Java client for the Notion API with a composable interceptor pipeline, automatic error mapping, and first-class support for every block type.

Java 17+ | Notion API 2026-03-11 | OkHttp + Jackson

---

## Authentication & Credentials

To use the Notion API, you need an **access token** (for internal integrations, this is called an Integration Secret) or an **OAuth access token** (for public integrations). Obtain these from the [Notion integrations dashboard](https://www.notion.so/my-integrations) after creating your integration. For OAuth, you will also need:

- **Client ID** and **Client Secret** (from the dashboard)
- **Redirect URIs** (set in your integration settings)

---

## Documentation

- [Getting Started](getting-started.md): Installation, client creation, first API call
- [Configuration](configuration.md): All builder options with defaults and examples
- [Pages Guide](guides/pages.md): Create, read, update, archive pages
- [Databases Guide](guides/databases.md): Create, query, update databases and schemas; filter and sort reference
- [Blocks Guide](guides/blocks.md): All 30+ block types, append/read/update/delete content
- [Users Guide](guides/users.md): List users, retrieve bot info
- [Search Guide](guides/search.md): Full-text search with filters and sorting
- [Comments Guide](guides/comments.md): Create and read page/block comments
- [Files & Media Guide](guides/files-and-media.md): Images, videos, PDFs, file attachments
- [Rich Text Guide](guides/rich-text.md): Compose and parse rich text with annotations, mentions, equations
- [Pagination Guide](guides/pagination.md): Cursor-based pagination for all list endpoints
- [Error Handling](advanced/error-handling.md): Exception hierarchy (10 typed exceptions), retry strategies
- [Interceptor Pipeline](advanced/interceptors.md): Architecture, built-in interceptors, writing custom ones
- [Exchange Logging](advanced/exchange-logging.md): Debug with request/response JSON files
- [Custom HTTP Client](advanced/custom-http-client.md): Replace OkHttp, MockWebServer testing
- [Notion Http Client](advanced/notion-http-client.md): Use NotionHttpClient.call() for direct endpoint access
- [Serialization](advanced/serialization.md): Jackson configuration, custom serializers
- [Sync External Data](recipes/sync-external-to-notion.md): Push CRM/issue tracker data into Notion
- [Notion as CMS](recipes/notion-as-cms.md): Read Notion content to power a website
- [Task Automation](recipes/task-automation.md): Automate project management workflows
- [Bulk Operations](recipes/bulk-operations.md): CSV import, database export, batch updates
- [Webhooks](recipes/webhooks.md): React to Notion changes in real-time
- [Development Setup](contributing/development-setup.md): Build, test, format commands
- [Architecture](contributing/architecture.md): Package design, pipeline, extension points
- [Testing Guide](contributing/testing-guide.md): Test doubles, MockWebServer, coverage
- [Release Process](contributing/release-process.md): Versioning, publishing, changelog

## Quick Reference

### Create a client from an auth header
```java
// Using a Bearer token from an HTTP header
NotionClient client = NotionClient.forToken("secret_abc123...");
// Or with the builder
NotionClient client = NotionClient.builder()
    .authToken("secret_abc123...")
    .build();
```

> **Note:** The Notion API expects a Bearer authentication header of the form `Bearer <token>`, where `<token>` is your **access token** (for internal integrations) or **OAuth access token** (for public integrations).

### Placeholder: Handling Notion OAuth Flow

<!-- TODO: Add a detailed chapter on implementing the OAuth 2.0 flow, obtaining/refreshing tokens, and using them with NotionClient. -->

### Make API calls (current low-level API)
```java
// GET
String json = client.getHttpClient().call("GET",
    ApiPath.builder("/pages/{id}").pathParam("id", pageId).build(),
    String.class);

// POST with body
Map<String, Object> body = Map.of("query", "search term");
String json = client.getHttpClient().call("POST",
    ApiPath.from("/search"), body, String.class);

// PATCH
Map<String, Object> update = Map.of("properties", Map.of(...));
String json = client.getHttpClient().call("PATCH",
    ApiPath.builder("/pages/{id}").pathParam("id", pageId).build(),
    update, String.class);
```

### Handle errors
```java
try {
    client.getHttpClient().call("GET", path, String.class);
} catch (NotFoundException e) {
    // 404 -- resource not found or not shared with integration
} catch (TooManyRequestsException e) {
    // 429 -- rate limited, retry after backoff
} catch (ValidationException e) {
    // 400 -- invalid request
} catch (NotionApiException e) {
    // any other Notion API error
    // e.getStatus(), e.getCode(), e.getMessage(), e.getRequestId()
} catch (UncheckedIOException e) {
    // network-level failure
}
```

### Exception hierarchy
- NotionApiException (base)
  - ValidationException (400)
  - UnauthorizedException (401)
  - ForbiddenException (403)
  - NotFoundException (404)
  - ConflictException (409)
  - TooManyRequestsException (429)
  - InternalServerException (500)
  - BadGatewayException (502)
  - ServiceUnavailableException (503)
  - GatewayTimeoutException (504)

### Builder options
| Option | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| authToken(String) | required | Notion integration token |
| version(String) | "2026-03-11" | Notion-Version header |
| baseUrl(String) | "https://api.notion.com/v1" | API base URL |
| rawHttpClient(HttpClient) | OkHttp, 30s timeouts | Custom HTTP transport |
| jsonSerializer(JsonSerializer) | JacksonSerializer.withDefaults() | Custom serializer |
| exchangeLogging(Path) | disabled | Write exchanges to disk |

### Key classes
- `NotionClient` -- entry point (forToken, builder)
- `NotionClientBuilder` -- fluent configuration
- `NotionHttpClient` -- typed HTTP client interface
- `ApiPath` -- URL template with path/query params
- `HttpClient` -- transport interface (HttpRequest, HttpResponse, Body types)
- `HttpClientInterceptor` -- beforeSend/afterReceive hooks
- `JsonSerializer` -- toJson/toObject interface
- `JacksonSerializer` -- Jackson implementation (withDefaults, pretty)
- `Block` -- base class for 30+ block subtypes
- `RichText` -- structured text with annotations
- `User`, `Person` -- user models
- `NotionApiException` -- base error with status, code, message, requestId

### Package structure
- `io.kristaxlab.notion` -- NotionClient, NotionClientBuilder
- `io.kristaxlab.notion.auth` -- token providers, auth interceptor
- `io.kristaxlab.notion.http` -- Notion HTTP client, version interceptor
- `io.kristaxlab.notion.http.base.client` -- HttpClient, ApiClient, OkHttp3Client
- `io.kristaxlab.notion.http.base.config` -- ApiClientConfig, ConfigKey
- `io.kristaxlab.notion.http.base.interceptor` -- interceptor interface, logging, recording
- `io.kristaxlab.notion.http.base.json` -- JsonSerializer, JacksonSerializer
- `io.kristaxlab.notion.http.base.request` -- ApiPath
- `io.kristaxlab.notion.http.error` -- NotionApiException hierarchy
- `io.kristaxlab.notion.model` -- BaseNotionObject, NotionError
- `io.kristaxlab.notion.model.block` -- Block + 30 subtypes
- `io.kristaxlab.notion.model.common` -- RichText, Parent, Icon, FileData
- `io.kristaxlab.notion.model.page` -- PageRequest
- `io.kristaxlab.notion.model.user` -- User, Person
